💝 Projects Donation ← Back to Schedule

ASI Southwest 2026 Mission Projects

A curated overview of the 2026 project opportunities and ministry initiatives from the official project packet.

FARM STEW

FARM STEW aims to fight extreme poverty and malnutrition in Africa—particularly in Uganda, South Sudan, and Zimbabwe—by training rural families in sustainable agriculture, nutrition, hygiene, and access to clean water.

Project funding supports a new area in Renk, South Sudan, with a three-year plan for hands-on farm training with three full-time workers, including worker training, transport, and supplies.

Southwestern Adventist University (SWAU)

Building on nearly two decades of service, SWAU is preparing another mission trip of 40 students to the Navajo Nation.

Funding supports construction and upkeep projects, health seminar outreach, Senior Center support, five days of service initiatives, and youth programming.

La Vida Mission

Serving the Navajo Nation for 60 years, La Vida Mission provides education through a K–8 boarding school, community outreach, free education, and clean water support.

Project funding will purchase a multi-use tractor for agricultural operations, road and sewer work, and vehicle recovery support.

Build and Restore International

Build and Restore International mobilizes volunteers worldwide to build and restore churches, schools, and community facilities.

Funds support renovation of a Houston-area learning center featuring a lifestyle center, trade academy, and farm with services including education, oxygen therapy, massage, and health coaching.

Narrow Way Ministries

Narrow Way Ministries is a full-spectrum faith ministry of message, mission, and music, focused on ministry to individuals seeking freedom from the LGBT+ lifestyle.

Project support enables a stand-alone server repository for testimonies connected to their documentary on religious liberty, freedom of speech, and conversion therapy.

Creation Illustrated

Creation Illustrated shares biblical truth and character-building lessons by highlighting the wonders of God’s creation and revealing insights into His wisdom, love, and design.

Funding supports distribution of current and back-issue magazines to prison inmates through chaplains and ministry partnerships.

Profeteia Ministries

Founded in Argentina, Profeteia Ministries proclaims the gospel and biblical prophecy through Spanish-language digital platforms with a broad audience.

Project funding will develop a free digital Bible study platform on twelve foundational Adventist teachings, guiding people toward connection with local SDA churches.

Ouachita Hills Academy (OHA)

OHA is a Seventh-day Adventist supporting ministry school in southwest Arkansas, focused on nurturing students in a relationship with Jesus and preparing them for service.

Project support will purchase multiple cameras for a campus security system to deter crime, protect students and staff, and improve accountability.

Ouachita Hills College (OHC)

OHC is a self-supporting Seventh-day Adventist educational organization providing Christ-centered, character-oriented education that equips students for profession and mission.

Funding goes toward cafeteria upgrades including new fans, a PA system, and wall sound treatment to support meals and other large gatherings.

Child Impact International

Child Impact International protects vulnerable children through education, prevention, and long-term care that addresses root causes of exploitation.

Project funds will support child-protection workshops, materials, and teaching resources for public schools and communities across Barreirinha, Brazil, including age-appropriate abuse prevention education over an 18-month period.

ASI Southwest • Spring Convention 2026 • Project Highlights
